_meta.title: Timberborn water / weather / terrain mechanics
_meta.purpose: Numeric knowledge base for the planner/controller to reason about drought survival, water storage sizing, irrigation, and contamination.
_meta.notes: Numbers marked with a sibling _uncertain:true are inferred from community guides or vary by patch. In-game a 'day' is 24h; a worker shift is ~16h, so per-day production is per-shift, not per-24h.

units.water: m^3 (1 tile x 1 tile x 1.0 depth == 1 water unit)
units.day: one in-game day; 16h work shift + rest
units.depth_height: 1.0 == one full block of water/terrain height

weather_cycle.definition: A cycle = one Temperate season followed by one Hazardous season (Drought for Folktails-visible, or Badtide). Water sources stop emitting (drought) or emit badwater (badtide) during the hazard.

temperate_length_days._note: Ranges from community cycle guides; official wiki lists first-event hazard durations but not temperate ranges explicitly.

later_drought_length_days_community._note: Typical mid/late-game ranges after the handicap has ramped up; from community guides.

scaling.mechanism: Hazard lengths grow each cycle via a duration handicap that expands from a starting fraction to 100% over an expansion window.
scaling._note: Hard handicap values from wiki; per-difficulty extra-days are community estimates.

hazard_type_selection.faction_note: Folktails campaigns default to droughts as the visible hazard; Ironteeth encounter badtides. Both mechanics exist in every map; badtide frequency is governed by the chance above.

water_pump.faction: Folktails (base water pump); Ironteeth analog exists

output_per_worker_action_water._note: per pumping action; scales with water depth under the intake

output_per_day_water._note: ~3 water/hour x 16h shift; ideal deep-water conditions. Lower in shallow water.

min_water_depth_to_pump._note: shallow water yields the low end of the per-action range; ~0.15-0.3 minimum

water_pump.badwater_penalty: Pumps regular water only; at higher badwater concentration efficiency drops toward zero.
water_pump.depth_matters: Deeper water under the intake -> faster pumping (toward the 1.0/action end). This is why reservoirs are built DEEP, not wide.

large_water_pump._note: higher throughput multi-worker version unlocked later

mechanical_water_pump._note: power-driven, no worker; throughput scales with power

deep_water_pump_ironteeth._note: Ironteeth deep pump; reaches deeper water

beaver_water_per_day._note: ~2.1 avg; ~2.25 for a beaver working a full 16h shift

large_water_tank._note: 4x medium, 40x small. Some older patches listed 300.

buffer_rule_of_thumb.example: 10 beavers x 2.25 x drought_days = required buffer if pumps produce nothing during the hazard.

resource_dumps_as_water._note: Fluid Dumps store/discard water but are not efficient day-buffer storage; tanks are the intended buffer.

dam.behavior: Weir: holds water up to ~0.5 above its base then overflows across the top. Used to raise river level while letting excess spill.
dam._note: Water flows OVER a dam; it does not fully seal.

levee.behavior: Solid wall, blocks a full 1.0 of water height per tile. Stackable: 3 high = 3.0 wall. Does NOT let water over the top until overtopped.

floodgate.behavior: Adjustable-height gate; raise/lower to hold or release water. Set target height to control reservoir release during drought.

sluice.behavior: Automated gate that opens/closes based on water level or contamination threshold; e.g. auto-dump badwater or pass water only below a set depth.

dynamite.behavior: Removes/lowers terrain blocks to deepen reservoirs or carve channels.
dynamite._note: Terrain depth for a reservoir is created by removing dirt (dynamite / platform digging), enabling deep narrow storage.

spread.via: normal fluid simulation - badwater flows and mixes like water

cleanup.soil_recovery: Contaminated soil clears once badwater recedes and clean water/rain washes it; timing not officially numeric.

barriers.irrigation_barrier: Blocks moisture/contamination spread through terrain between tiles; used to protect farmland from badwater seep.
barriers.contamination_barrier: Prevents contamination crossing; pair with levees/sluices to keep a clean reservoir.

moisture_range_tiles_flat._note: Horizontal moisture spread from a water/moisture source on flat ground; commonly cited ~6-8 tiles.

irrigation.elevation_penalty: Each +1 block of elevation above the water source reduces irrigation range by ~6 blocks.

pollution_effect._note: Water above 50% pollution does not irrigate and instead contaminates terrain.

irrigation.why_crops_need_moisture: Every tile has a moisture level; crops and trees only grow on moist soil. During drought the water recedes, moisture decays from the edges inward, and unirrigated crops/trees die. Wide-but-shallow water dries fast; deep reservoirs + canals keep soil moist longer.
irrigation.channel_tip: 3-wide channels minimize evaporation while maximizing irrigation reach.

evaporation_value_by_adjacency._note: Higher adjacency (more surrounding water) => lower evaporation multiplier. Exposed narrow water evaporates fastest.

evaporation.implication: Deep + narrow-surface reservoirs lose the least water per day; this is the core drought-engineering lever.

drought_survival_planner_hints.core_principle: During drought, sources stop; you live on stored water (tanks) + whatever pumps can still lift from the shrinking reservoir. Reservoir loses ~base_evap/day plus pump draw.
drought_survival_planner_hints.min_first_drought_buffer_formula: buffer_water >= beavers * 2.25 * drought_days (fully via tanks if pumps can't reach water)

recommended_first_drought.assumptions: 8-12 beavers, normal difficulty, first drought ~2-3 days
recommended_first_drought.tanks: 1 Large Water Tank (1200) is overkill for the first drought; 1-2 Small Tanks (30 each) + a filled reservoir usually suffice.
recommended_first_drought.pumps: 1 Water Pump on water >=2 deep keeps producing through a short drought if the reservoir doesn't drop below its intake.
recommended_first_drought.reservoir: Dam/levee the river into a deep pond BEFORE the first drought so pumps stay submerged.
